title: Coniogramme caudata
intro: Coniogramme caudata, also known as Japanese fern or wild fern, is a type of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Coniogramme caudata contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions.
taxonomy: Coniogramme caudata is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Coniogramme, which contains several species of ferns. The family Pteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns, with many species found worldwide. Coniogramme caudata is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and the Pacific. The classification of Coniogramme caudata reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the Pteridaceae family.
